title: The One with the Free Porn

jokes.text: Maybe he found your flip-flop.
jokes.setup: Something disgusting is coming up from the shower drain
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion that a flip-flop might be causing the blockage
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about drain blockages. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Maybe we shouldn't pay our phone bill. Free phone sex. Maybe we shouldn't pay our gas bill.
jokes.setup: Joey theorizes that not paying cable bill led to free porn as punishment
jokes.punchline: Logical extension that not paying other bills might yield other 'punishments'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Joey's signature naive logic. The gas bill implication is darker humor.

jokes.text: Hey, that lady's all kinds of naked.
jokes.setup: The group discovers they have free porn
jokes.punchline: Joey's childlike, matter-of-fact observation about nudity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey line - innocent phrasing for adult content. 'All kinds of naked' is memorably awkward phrasing.

jokes.text: Finding money with naked people on it! But I made the mistake of turning off the TV. I never got it back again. And I'm sad.
jokes.setup: Comparing free porn to finding money
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple, childlike expression of genuine sadness about losing free porn
jokes.explanation: Joey's earnest sadness over losing porn is perfectly in character. The simple 'And I'm sad' is endearingly pathetic.

jokes.text: Why would he turn off the TV?
jokes.setup: Joey's story about losing free porn by turning off the TV
jokes.punchline: Chandler's incredulous question treating this as incomprehensible
jokes.explanation: Chandler's dry delivery sells the absurdity. Good economy - maximum confusion in few words.

jokes.text: And no one touches the air around the TV.
jokes.setup: Establishing rules to preserve the free porn
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd level of protecting even the air around TV
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from reasonable to absurd. Shows the paranoia building around preserving the porn.

jokes.text: Imagine a protective porn bubble, if you will.
jokes.setup: Continuing the escalation of protecting the TV
jokes.punchline: Formal, scientific language applied to protecting pornography
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - overly formal language for ridiculous concept. 'If you will' makes it sound academic. Very quotable.

jokes.text: We still have porn.
jokes.setup: Monica mutes the TV, potentially risking the free porn
jokes.punchline: Joey's relieved confirmation that the essential element remains
jokes.explanation: Joey's priorities are perfectly clear. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: I need a job with a smaller table. Or a job without a table. You mean, like a doctor?
jokes.setup: Phoebe complaining about lugging around massage table
jokes.punchline: Monica's suggestion that 'job without table' describes being a doctor
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection - Monica takes Phoebe's complaint about tables as career advice about medical profession.

jokes.text: You're blocking the porn!
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Monica talking while standing in front of TV
jokes.punchline: Joey's priority - they're interfering with porn viewing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - completely ignoring their conversation to focus on porn access. Great economy.

jokes.text: You must feel horrible. Hey, the guys have free porn.
jokes.setup: Monica sympathizing with Ross about Emily leaving
jokes.punchline: Immediate topic change to free porn as if it's relevant comfort
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition between emotional sympathy and random porn announcement. Shows how the porn has consumed everyone's attention.

jokes.text: Don't do the accent. It really bums me out.
jokes.setup: Ross starting to imitate Emily's British accent while quoting her
jokes.punchline: Chandler's specific, emotional request to stop the accent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Chandler's unexpected emotional reaction to Ross's bad accent work.

jokes.text: Is that why you hung out with that Ukrainian kid in school?
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining his fantasy about falling in love with a foreigner
jokes.punchline: Ross connecting this to a specific childhood memory
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - reveals Chandler's long-standing pattern while Ross shows his memory for embarrassing details.

jokes.text: Yeah, plus his mom used to put sour cream on everything.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about Ukrainian kid motivation
jokes.punchline: Chandler admitting secondary motivation was the food
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail - Chandler's honesty about being motivated by food as much as romance fantasy.

jokes.text: I am a good kisser. Then you two can sneak into the cockpit.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing romantic airport reunion scenario
jokes.punchline: Ross's random confidence boost, then Chandler escalating to sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Ross's random self-affirmation is mildly funny, Chandler's escalation predictable but functional.

jokes.text: I've been watching too much porn.
jokes.setup: Chandler's fantasy getting increasingly sexual
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that porn is affecting his thinking
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment connecting the porn subplot to current conversation. Clean punchline.

jokes.text: Let's take care of this one. If I get pregnant again, I have your card.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking about multiple births
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treating triplets like a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic - treating pregnancy like repeat business. Shows her practical approach to surrogacy.

jokes.text: You guys were worried I wouldn't even have one.
jokes.setup: Doctor confirming three heartbeats
jokes.punchline: Phoebe pointing out the irony of going from concern to abundance
jokes.explanation: Decent ironic observation about going from worried to overwhelmed. Functional character moment.

jokes.text: It'll be like one of those log rides when they come shooting out.
jokes.setup: Phoebe imagining three babies inside her
jokes.punchline: Comparing birth to amusement park water ride
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe moment - completely unexpected comparison that's both visual and slightly disturbing. Perfect character integration of her unique worldview.

jokes.text: What do you know? Have you ever had triplets? No. I'm sorry, I haven't. I'm going to use this a lot.
jokes.setup: Doctor trying to reassure Phoebe about triplet birth
jokes.punchline: Phoebe dismissing medical expertise, then promising to weaponize this comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - dismissing expertise with flawless logic, then planning to overuse her victory. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: You know how, when you're walking down the street... and you see three people in a row. And you say 'Oh, that's nice.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to break triplets news gently
jokes.punchline: Bizarre analogy that makes no sense as preparation
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe non-sequitur. The analogy is completely unhelpful but perfectly matches her thought process.

jokes.text: I've finally got my band!
jokes.setup: Learning about having triplets
jokes.punchline: Frank's first thought is forming a musical group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank Jr. - completely missing the parenting implications to focus on music dreams. Great economy.

jokes.text: No, back to happy. Back to happy!
jokes.setup: Alice mentioning the challenges of raising triplets
jokes.punchline: Frank desperately trying to return to positive emotions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's emotional immaturity and desire to avoid difficult realities.

jokes.text: It'll be like my very own little sweatshop.
jokes.setup: Alice planning to have students make baby clothes
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully describing child labor using exploitative terminology
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Alice's cheerful obliviousness. The sweatshop comparison is unexpectedly harsh.

jokes.text: You're in college? Refrigerator college.
jokes.setup: Frank mentioning he's in college
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a specialized trade school for appliance repair
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Frank's limited educational aspirations. 'Refrigerator college' is amusing specificity.

jokes.text: And I love refrigerators.
jokes.setup: Explaining his career choice
jokes.punchline: Simple, earnest declaration of appliance passion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank sincerity. The simple statement reveals his uncomplicated worldview.

jokes.text: Three kids and no money.
jokes.setup: Frank calling having three kids 'a different kind of dream'
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of their financial reality
jokes.explanation: Darkly honest assessment delivered with Frank's matter-of-fact style. Good economy.

jokes.text: That's a big candy bar.
jokes.setup: Ross approaching Emily at airport with something large
jokes.punchline: Emily's underwhelmed reaction to what Ross brought
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about Ross bringing inadequate airport gift. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Thank you.
jokes.setup: Ross declares his love at the airport
jokes.punchline: Emily's completely inappropriate response to love declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Two simple words completely deflate romantic gesture. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: It's no problem.
jokes.setup: Emily's 'thank you' response to love declaration
jokes.punchline: Ross's equally inappropriate response treating love like a favor
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of cringe. Ross doubling down on treating love declaration like customer service.

jokes.text: It's the theme from Good Will Humping.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about music from the porn
jokes.punchline: Joey's porn parody of 'Good Will Hunting'
jokes.explanation: Decent porn title parody. Shows Joey's familiarity with adult entertainment naming conventions.

jokes.text: Yeah, he likes porn.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing Joshua's negative opinions about dirty movies
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler deadpan. Cuts through Rachel's naivety with blunt male perspective.

jokes.text: To find out if he really thinks models are too skinny.
jokes.setup: Rachel going to test Joshua's stated preferences
jokes.punchline: Revealing her plan to test his honesty about beauty standards
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's insecurity and need to test boyfriend's honesty. Functional character moment.

jokes.text: Frank has to quit college... because his super-fertile sister's having three babies.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining Frank's college situation
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description that highlights the family weirdness
jokes.explanation: 'Super-fertile sister' is amusingly clinical description of very weird family situation.

jokes.text: But I can get you free White-Out.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about insider trading information
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pathetic offer of office supplies instead
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between criminal insider trading and petty office supply theft. Shows Chandler's actual level of corporate access.

jokes.text: She said, 'Thank you.' I said, 'I love you.' And she said, 'Thank you.'
jokes.setup: Ross explaining what happened at airport
jokes.punchline: Repetition emphasizing the awkward response
jokes.explanation: Good use of repetition to emphasize the cringe. Structure builds the awkwardness through repetition.

jokes.text: She already lives in London.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggesting Ross play hard to get
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out the geographic impossibility
jokes.explanation: Logical objection to romantic advice. Shows Ross's practical thinking in crisis.

jokes.text: So you go to Tokyo.
jokes.setup: Ross pointing out Emily already lives far away
jokes.punchline: Chandler's solution is to go even further away
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and absurd logic. Perfect economy - maximum ridiculousness in three words.

jokes.text: You're over! What? You know.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to deliver comeback
jokes.punchline: Failing to complete the insult and then pretending it made sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - attempting sophisticated comeback, failing, then doubling down. Shows his intellectual insecurity.

jokes.text: Good one.
jokes.setup: Ross's failed comeback attempt
jokes.punchline: Joey's sarcastic congratulation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective sarcasm. Joey recognizing Ross's failure with minimal words.

jokes.text: Then we'd be the guys who turned off free porn.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting they turn off the porn
jokes.punchline: Chandler explaining the social consequences
jokes.explanation: Shows the mythology that's built up around the free porn. Good character insight into male psychology.

jokes.text: Look, I'm going to have a kid someday. Okay? And someday that kid is going to ask me if I ever turned off free porn. I don't wanna have to tell him that I did.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they can't turn off the porn
jokes.punchline: Joey's elaborate justification involving future parental integrity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic - treating free porn as a moral imperative for future fatherhood. Perfect character integration and highly quotable absurdist reasoning.

jokes.text: Did you ever asked you dad that? I don't want to talk about it.
jokes.setup: Joey's theory about fathers and free porn
jokes.punchline: Implication that Chandler actually had this conversation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that implies Chandler's family weirdness extends to this area. Joey's avoidance suggests embarrassing truth.

jokes.text: Don't you hate to cut a tin can with an ordinary steak knife?
jokes.setup: Phoebe demonstrating kitchen knives
jokes.punchline: Addressing a problem no one has
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of infomercial sales pitches. Shows Phoebe's gullibility for get-rich-quick schemes.

jokes.text: Pregnant woman slays four?
jokes.setup: Joey asking about her knife demonstration
jokes.punchline: Phoebe imagining violent news headline about herself
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Phoebe's characteristic morbid imagination. Good economy and unexpectedly violent imagery.

jokes.text: What's that? My Saturn dealership.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her money-making plan
jokes.punchline: Revealing an even more unrealistic business venture
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from knife selling to car dealership. Shows Phoebe's completely unrealistic business planning.

jokes.text: Does that mean the same thing in England as it does in America?
jokes.setup: Emily saying 'there's someone else'
jokes.punchline: Ross hoping cultural differences might save him
jokes.explanation: Good desperate logic from Ross. Shows his denial and hope for linguistic loopholes.

jokes.text: When you say 'still around,' you mean still around like you might be in a relationship... or still around like you might have died waiting for the phone to ring?
jokes.setup: Ross saying he told Emily to call when she decides
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifying the ambiguity with morbid option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - taking Ross's casual phrase and revealing darker interpretation. Good structure contrasting two meanings.

jokes.text: I missed and hit the door. But it opened really hard.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he put his fist through a wall
jokes.punchline: Revealing he actually just hit a door that opened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - trying to seem tough but revealing pathetic truth. The 'opened really hard' justification is beautifully weak.

jokes.text: I made fun of his accent. You sat back and let him have her. You didn't fight. I just told you, I made fun of him behind his back.
jokes.setup: Joey asking what Ross did about Paolo
jokes.punchline: Ross insisting that mockery constituted fighting
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Ross defending his passive-aggressive approach as actual fighting. Shows his avoidance of confrontation.

jokes.text: It's like a snake pit. That could be you and Emily. That but nicer.
jokes.setup: Joey pointing at the porn on TV
jokes.punchline: Describing porn as romantic inspiration with qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic using porn as romantic template. The 'but nicer' shows awareness it's not perfect model.

jokes.text: Can you pick me up one of those Toblerone bars at the airport?
jokes.setup: Ross deciding to fly to London for Emily
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate focus on airport chocolate
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - missing emotional weight to focus on food opportunity. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: A place that no one will get out of alive?
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing massage taxi combination
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out the safety implications
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about the impracticality of mobile massage. Monica's practical concerns about safety.

jokes.text: It's a Relaxi-Taxi!
jokes.setup: Describing the massage taxi concept
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's proud portmanteau business name
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that sounds professional but ridiculous. Very quotable and shows Phoebe's business enthusiasm.

jokes.text: You came up with Relaxi-Cab. That's not good.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming credit for the name
jokes.punchline: Revealing his version was actually worse
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Frank's inferior contribution. Shows the collaborative creative process gone wrong.

jokes.text: It's not Relaxi-Cab. It's Relaxi-Cab. Like Taxi-Cab. That is better.
jokes.setup: Defending Frank's version
jokes.punchline: Phoebe explaining why the same name is actually different and better
jokes.explanation: Shows Phoebe's ability to convince herself of distinctions without differences. Mild confusion humor.

jokes.text: I was right! I'm right, right?
jokes.setup: Emily appearing at the apartment
jokes.punchline: Monica's excited vindication of her romantic predictions
jokes.explanation: Good Monica moment showing her need to be right about relationship predictions. Character-appropriate self-satisfaction.

jokes.text: Yes, Joey, you can have all the chocolate you want.
jokes.setup: Emily leaving emotional message for Ross
jokes.punchline: Interruption to address Joey's chocolate request
jokes.explanation: Good interruption of romantic moment with Joey's food priorities. Shows how his needs intrude on others' moments.

jokes.text: Mrs. Newman, if you're listening, bugger off. None of your business.
jokes.setup: Emily leaving message on answering machine
jokes.punchline: Addressing nosy neighbor presumably listening to message
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail about British neighbor dynamics. Shows Emily's awareness of privacy invasion.

jokes.text: Thank you.
jokes.setup: Emily's love declaration on answering machine
jokes.punchline: Ross repeating the same inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure. Ross hasn't learned and responds identically to answering machine. Excellent economy and cringe factor.

jokes.text: I was just at the bank. There was this really hot teller... and she didn't ask me to do it with her in the vault.
jokes.setup: Guys realizing porn has affected their expectations
jokes.punchline: Chandler's disappointment about realistic bank interaction
jokes.explanation: Good observation about porn warping reality expectations. Shows how fantasy affects mundane interactions.

jokes.text: No 'Nice apartment, bet the bedrooms are huge'? Nothing!
jokes.setup: Joey describing pizza delivery disappointment
jokes.punchline: Specific pickup line Joey expected from delivery person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - having specific dialogue expectations from adult films. Shows his complete immersion in porn logic.

jokes.text: Free porn! We have free porn!
jokes.setup: Testing if porn is still available after turning TV on/off
jokes.punchline: Ecstatic celebration of porn's return
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the episode-long tension about losing free porn. Shows their genuine relief and joy.

jokes.text: Don't worry, I won't let the other two know.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he likes the middle triplet best
jokes.punchline: Promising to keep his preference secret from the unborn babies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - treating ultrasound images like people with feelings. Dark humor about playing favorites with unborn children.

tmdb_overview: Chandler and Joey discover free pornographic channels. Monica encourages Ross to tell Emily he loves her before she leaves. Phoebe receives shocking news from her doctor.
